# Golden file: database.postgresql pack (E1)
# 25 cases: 8 destructive-high, 6 destructive-medium, 11 safe/edge
format: v1
---
# D1: DROP DATABASE (High)
command: psql -c "DROP DATABASE myapp"
decision: Ask
severity: High
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-drop-database
---
# D1: DROP DATABASE with host flag (no env escalation; env detection uses env vars)
command: psql -h localhost -c "DROP DATABASE production"
decision: Ask
severity: High
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-drop-database
---
# D1: dropdb utility (no env escalation — "production" in "myapp_production"
# has no word boundary due to underscore)
command: dropdb myapp_production
decision: Ask
severity: High
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: dropdb
---
# D2: DROP TABLE
command: psql -c "DROP TABLE users"
decision: Ask
severity: High
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-drop-table
---
# D2: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS
command: psql -c "DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sessions"
decision: Ask
severity: High
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-drop-table
---
# D3: TRUNCATE
command: psql -c "TRUNCATE TABLE orders"
decision: Ask
severity: High
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-truncate
---
# D4: DROP TABLE case insensitive
command: psql -c "drop table users"
decision: Ask
severity: High
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-drop-table
---
# D5: DROP SCHEMA
command: psql -c "DROP SCHEMA public CASCADE"
decision: Ask
severity: High
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-drop-schema
---
# D6: DELETE FROM without WHERE (Medium)
command: psql -c "DELETE FROM users"
decision: Ask
severity: Medium
confidence: Medium
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-delete-no-where
---
# D7: UPDATE without WHERE (Medium)
command: psql -c "UPDATE users SET active=false"
decision: Ask
severity: Medium
confidence: Medium
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-update-no-where
---
# D8: pg_dump --clean
command: pg_dump --clean mydb > backup.sql
decision: Ask
severity: Medium
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: pg-dump-clean
---
# D8: pg_dump -c short flag
command: pg_dump -c mydb > backup.sql
decision: Ask
severity: Medium
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: pg-dump-clean
---
# D9: pg_restore --clean
command: pg_restore --clean backup.dump
decision: Ask
severity: Medium
confidence: High
pack: database.postgresql
rule: pg-restore-clean
---
# D10: ALTER TABLE ... DROP
command: psql -c "ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN email"
decision: Ask
severity: Medium
confidence: Medium
pack: database.postgresql
rule: psql-alter-drop
---
# S1: SELECT is safe
command: psql -c "SELECT * FROM users"
decision: Allow
---
# S2: meta-command \dt is safe
command: psql -c "\dt"
decision: Allow
---
# S3: meta-command \d+ is safe
command: psql -c "\d+ users"
decision: Allow
---
# S4: pg_dump without --clean is safe
command: pg_dump mydb > backup.sql
decision: Allow
---
# S5: createdb is safe
command: createdb myapp_test
decision: Allow
---
# S6: psql interactive is safe
command: psql -h localhost mydb
decision: Allow
---
# S7: pg_restore without --clean is safe
command: pg_restore backup.dump
decision: Allow
---
# S8: DELETE FROM with WHERE is safe (no destructive match)
command: psql -c "DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 1"
decision: Allow
---
# S9: UPDATE with WHERE is safe (no destructive match)
command: psql -c "UPDATE users SET active=false WHERE id=1"
decision: Allow
---
# S10: psql -f file execution (no match — file content not inspected)
command: psql -f migrate.sql
decision: Allow
---
# S11: EXPLAIN is safe
command: psql -c "EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM users"
decision: Allow
